item.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a full field of 20 horses in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: Who are the favorites, longshots heading into Kentucky Derby?
item.description: Saturday marks the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs. 20 horses are set to run for the roses in the first leg of the triple crown. Hannah Vanbiber, a senior editor at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to preview what's dubbed the most exciting two minutes in sports.

item.title: Tennessee, Alabama take steps to redraw House maps in wake of Supreme Court ruling
item.description: The Republican governors of Tennessee and Alabama called state lawmakers into special sessions on Friday, initial steps in what could be a scramble to redraw congressional maps after the Supreme Court narrowed the Voting Rights Act.

item.title: 3 months after Nancy Guthrie's disappearance, where things stand
item.description: Friday marked exactly three months since Nancy Guthrie, the mother of "Today" show co-host Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing. Briana Whitney, a breaking news reporter for the Crime Junkie podcast, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

item.title: How are Americans prioritizing spending amid rising costs?
item.description: Consumers are cutting back on goods with sharp price increases, according to the Bureau of Economic Analysis. From December to February, spending on clothing fell 7%, spending on furniture fell 5% and spending on sports equipment fell 6%. The Wall Street Journal economics reporter Rachel Wolfe writes in her recent piece that recent inflation is not driven by consumer demand, but by companies passing on increased costs to customers. She joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: WHCA scholarship recipients discuss dinner shooting: "I started just praying for everyone's safety"
item.description: For more than 100 years, the White House Correspondents' Dinner has celebrated the First Amendment and freedom of the press. The annual event is also a fundraiser for journalism scholarships. This year, the White House Correspondents' Association awarded $156,000 in grants to 30 college students. Two recipients, Kaitlin Bender-Thomas and Madison Maynard, join "The Daily Report" to discuss the shooting.

item.title: Exxon Mobil, Chevron earnings fall, but top revenue expectations
item.description: Earnings reports for Exxon Mobil and Chevron showed a decrease in profits during the last fiscal quarter due to Iran war disruptions. Despite the loss on paper, both U.S. oil giants topped Wall Street expectations when it came to revenue for the three month period ending March 31. CBS News business contributor Javier David has more.

item.title: After a fire torched their Nebraska ranch, anonymous donors came to their rescue
item.description: Mike and Kayla Wintz lost their entire 11,000-acre ranch to a wildfire in the span of about two hours. They have since been gifted about $80,000 worth of hay, mostly from anonymous donors.

item.title: Videos raise new questions about D.C. dinner shooting
item.description: Newly released video shows the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect and his movements ahead of the attack. Other video also shows a clearer image of the confrontation between law enforcement and the suspect. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more details.

item.title: ICE reports 18th detainee death in 2026, putting agency on track for new record
item.description: ICE reported the 18th death of an individual in its custody so far this year, putting the agency on track to record a new all-time high in detainee deaths.

item.title: Body found in Tampa Bay identified as 2nd missing doctoral student
item.description: Nahida Bristy and Zamil Limon were last seen​ in the Tampa area on April 16. Limon's roommate has been charged with their murders.

item.title: Judge rebukes prosecutors in hearing for accused correspondents' dinner gunman
item.description: A federal judge scolded prosecutors for pushing to move forward with detention proceedings for accused correspondents' dinner gunman Cole Allen, even though Allen agreed to remain in custody.

item.title: May Day ralliers protest outside ICE detention facility in downtown Los Angeles
item.description: Thousands took to the streets in Los Angeles on Friday to participate in May Day rallies for workers' rights. CBS News Los Angeles reporter Luzdelia Caballero has more.

item.title: Trump tells Congress "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated"
item.description: President Trump on Friday told Congress that hostilities with Iran have "terminated," addressing a critical 60-day deadline.

item.title: Deaths in ICE custody set to hit new record this year
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement reported another death of one of its detainees, putting the total of people who have died in ICE custody this year at 18. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: Donors pitch in to help Nebraska ranchers who lost everything
item.description: The largest wildfire in Nebraska history burned about a thousand square miles of ranchland, including the Wintz's entire 11,000-acre ranch. When they and others faced the threat of losing their livelihoods, thousands of anonymous donors stepped up from across the U.S.

item.title: Missing Florida doctoral student's body found
item.description: A body found in Tampa Bay has been identified as the second missing student from the University of South Florida, according to the sheriff, who called their killings a "monstrous crime." Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: New video shows correspondents' dinner suspect before shooting
item.description: According to investigators, a gunman was inside the D.C. Hilton last Friday, waiting for his chance to assassinate President Trump and members of his Cabinet. New video offers the clearest view yet of what happened just before the attack. Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: Trump says Iran hostilities are over despite continued military presence, blockade
item.description: Over 50,000 U.S. military personnel remain deployed across the Middle East and a naval blockade is crippling the Iranian economy. But in a letter Friday, President Trump told lawmakers that "the hostilities that began" in late February "have terminated." Ed O'Keefe has more.

item.title: Appeals court blocks mailing of abortion pill mifepristone in U.S.
item.description: A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.

item.title: Investigators believe stolen vape pen caused Washington state high school stabbings
item.description: After multiple stabbings on Thursday at a high school in Tacoma, Washington, a student is facing multiple assault charges. Five people were wounded, including the suspect, and investigators believe it all started over a stolen vape pen.

item.title: Spirit Airlines poised to cease operations as soon as Saturday, barring last minute intervention
item.description: Spirit Airlines is essentially out of available cash, so barring last-minute intervention, the company is poised to cease operations as soon as Saturday morning, potentially stranding thousands of fliers. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: What would a Spirit Airlines shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights if the budget airline ceases operations this weekend.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Japan attacks Pearl Harbor in 1941
item.description: In the 20th century, no event rocked America more than the surprise Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces a very particular part of vital CBS News Radio coverage from that day.

item.title: Pickleball Inc. $225 million deal creates new home for sport
item.description: The deal merged Major League Pickleball and the Carvana PPA Tour, two of the nascent sport's most active entities, under one company, Pickleball Inc.

item.title: "Game of chicken" between U.S., Iran to continue for now, national security analyst says
item.description: Iran has sent the U.S. its revised proposal to end the war. The latest offer came as a top Iranian commander said any U.S. attack would draw a "painful retaliation." President Trump addressed the negotiations earlier on Friday. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Trump administration to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said earlier this week he was reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, which hosts more than 35,000 U.S. service members.

item.title: More Americans are quitting their U.S. jobs to work abroad, study finds
item.description: Since 2021, the share of U.S.-based employees who have left their jobs to work in another country has more than doubled.

item.title: Could the FCC really yank ABC's TV licenses amid Trump spat with Kimmel?
item.description: Federal telecom regulators can revoke broadcast licenses, but legal experts say the FCC would face a tough road in forcing ABC to go dark.

item.title: Louisiana Rep. Troy Carter calls Supreme Court map decision a "gut punch" to democracy
item.description: Louisiana is suspending its May 16 House primaries following the Supreme Court's decision to strike down its congressional map. The high court found the state relied too heavily on race when it created a second majority-Black district. Democratic Rep. Troy Carter of Louisiana joins "The Takeout" to discuss the ruling.
